data.title: Dentsu Non-GAAP EPS of ¥162.81; initiates mid-term management plan from FY2025 to FY2027
data.description:
• Dentsu press release (OTCPK:DNTUF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of ¥162.81.
  • Revenue of ¥335.8B (+4.2% Y/Y).
  • FY2025 guidance; the Group organic growth is expected to be 1%. We target operating margins at 12%, a decline compared to FY2024, mainly due to upfront internal investments to restore competitiveness.
  • Key financial targets to be achieved by FY2027: 1. Organic growth: 4.0% 2. Operating margin: 16-17% 3. Operating cash flow for the year: 140 billion yen 4. ROE: mid-teens range.

data.title: Norsk Hydro to propose cash dividend of NOK 2.25 a share
data.description:
• Norsk Hydro (OTCQX:NHYDY) has decided to propose to the general meeting a dividend of NOK 2.25/share dividend.
  • Payable May 20; for shareholders of record May 13; ex-div May 13.
  • See NHYDY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Eutelsat Communications reports 1H results; reaffirms FY 2024–25 objectives
data.description:
• Eutelsat Communications press release (OTCPK:EUTLF): 1H Revenue of €606.2M (+5.9% Y/Y).
  • Operating verticals revenues of €600m up 3.9% like-for-like.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin of 55.2%.
  • First Half Video revenues were down by 6.4% to €309.2 million, in line with the broader secular market decline.
  • Total Connectivity revenues for the First Half of FY 2024-25 stood at €290.7 million, up by 21.1% on a reported basis and up by 17.8% like-for-like.
  • First Half Fixed Connectivity revenues stood at €118.9 million, up 22.2% year-on-year.
  • First Half Government Services revenues stood at €96.4 million, up by 21.9% year-on-year.
  • First Half Mobile Connectivity revenues stood at €75.3 million, up 7.1% year-on-year.
  • The backlog stood at €3.7 billion on 31 December 2024 versus €3.9 billion a year earlier.
  • FY 2024–25 objectives confirmed; Gross capital expenditure in FY 2024-25 initially expected in a range of €700-800 million euros is now expected c.€200 lower, in a range of €500-600 million euros.
  • Eutelsat also continues to target leverage of c.3x in the medium term.

data.title: Kovo+ signs letter of intent to acquire exclusive rights to AI Vector and Veebas
data.description:
• Kovo+ Holdings (TSXV:KOVO:CA) has entered into a non-binding letter of intent with Avonlea Ventures #2 to acquire certain contractual assets of AVI, it said Thursday.
  • The aggregate purchase price is $5 million.
  • AVI is an Ontario corporation controlled by Michael Steele, a current director and controlling indirect shareholder of the company.

data.title: Northpointe Bancshares prices IPO at $14.50 per share
data.description:
Northpointe Bancshares (NPB) a Michigan corporation and the bank holding company for Northpointe Bank, a wholly owned subsidiary and a Michigan non-member bank, priced 10.42M shares at a price to the public of $14.50 per share, including 8.62M shares offered by Northpointe and 1.8M shares by certain shareholders of Northpointe.

The underwriters will have a 30-day option to purchase up to 2,574 additional shares of common stock from Northpointe and ~1.56M additional shares of common stock from the selling shareholders, at the initial public offering price.

The shares of Northpointe common stock are expected to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol “NPB” on February 14, 2025.

The offering is expected to close on February 18, 2025.

Northpointe will receive net proceeds of approximately $114.4M and intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes, which may include growing its existing lines of business or using a portion to redeem all, or a portion, of its preferred stock.

data.title: Norsk Hydro Non-GAAP EPS of NOK 1.11, revenue of NOK 55.06B
data.description:
• Norsk Hydro press release (OTCQX:NHYDY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of NOK 1.11.
  • Revenue of NOK 55.06B (+17.8% Y/Y).
  • Hydro’s adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024 was NOK 7,701 million, up from NOK 3,737 million in the same quarter last year.
  • Hydro generated NOK 1.7 billion in free cash flow during the fourth quarter and 2024 adjusted RoaCE ended at 8.5 percent.
  • Adjusted net debt decreased from NOK 25.0 billion to NOK 24.1 billion.

data.title: DBGI prices $7.5M stock offering
data.description:
Digital Brands Group (OTC:DBGI) priced its public offering of an aggregate of 11.36M units, of which each unit includes a share of common stock and/or pre-funded warrant to purchase common stock, plus two common stock purchase warrants, at a public offering price of $0.66 per unit.

The gross proceeds are expected to be $7.5M.

DBG intends to use the net proceeds of the offering for working capital, general corporate purposes, and the repayment of debt.

The offering is expected to close on February 18, 2025.

data.title: Asia markets mixed as investors assess Trump reciprocal tariffs plan, PBOC's latest policy signals
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets mixed on Friday following the rally on Wall Street Thursday after US President Donald Trump delayed implementing reciprocal tariffs and hopes of easing the trade war following the Modi-Trump talks capped the falls.

The market sentiment remained cautious following the People's Bank of China's latest policy signals.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 0.85% to around 39,300 on Friday, ending a three-day rally as the yen sharply rebounded overnight. The Japanese yen traded around 153 per dollar on Friday after surging about 1% in the previous session.

The investors continued to navigate Japan's busy earnings season, which has yielded mixed results so far.

Meanwhile, Japan’s Economy Minister, Ryosei Akazawa, stated on Friday that Japan would respond appropriately to any US reciprocal tariffs.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.04% to around 3,337, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.9% to 10,720 on Friday, recovering from losses in the previous session after the People’s Bank of China pledged to support the economy with loose monetary policy. The offshore yuan weakened around 7.29 per dollar, giving back gains from the previous session.

The central bank also forecast stable economic growth for China in 2025, as stimulus measures implemented last year begin to revive production, demand, and market sentiment.

The central bank pledged to ease monetary policy when appropriate to support economic growth, emphasizing liquidity management and targeted measures to stabilize the financial system.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 2.12% to 22,196 in Friday's morning session, recovering from slight losses in the previous session amid broad sector gains.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.58% to 75,832, touching their nearly three-week low in early deals on Friday, after being flat in the previous session. Almost all sectors traded in the red, led by pharmaceuticals.

Total passenger vehicle sales in India grew 3.5% year-over-year to 351,310 units in January 2025, following an 11.4% jump in the previous month, according to data from the Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (SIAM).

Australia (AS51) rose 0.19% to around 8,590 on Friday, reaching fresh all-time highs. The Australian dollar rose above $0.632 on Friday, approaching two-month highs.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended higher after President Trump sparked hopes about resolving the Ukraine conflict and delayed a decision on imposing additional tariffs on imported goods.

Meanwhile, the latest producer inflation report came in hotter-than-expected, following strong consumer inflation data the day before. However, the components suggest that the core PCE inflation, which the Fed favors, will likely come in lower than anticipated later this month.

U.S. stock futures remained steady on Friday after President Donald Trump signed a memorandum to review reciprocal tariffs but postponed taking immediate action, while hinting at possible auto tariffs: Dow +0.04%; S&P 500 +0.09%; Nasdaq +0.04%.

Additionally, market participants awaited US retail sales data on Friday to assess the strength of the economy.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

China’s services sector growth slows to four-month low in January

Japan’s Jan services PMI revised upward to 53; real wages rises for 2nd consecutive month

Trump in 'no hurry' to speak to China's Xi Jinping over escalating trade tensions

U.S. will delay Canada tariffs as countries work on beefing up border security

data.title: TikTok back live on Google and Apple app stores in U.S.
Cementos Pacasmayo S.A.A. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Cementos Pacasmayo S.A.A. press release (NYSE:CPAC): Q4 sales volume of cement, concrete and precast increased by 2.6%.
  • Revenues increased by 3.0%, in line with increased sales volume.
  • Consolidated EBITDA of S/142.5 million, an 8.7% decrease compared to adjusted EBITDA in 4Q23.
  • Consolidated EBITDA margin of 27.1%, a 3.5 percentage point decrease compared to adjusted EBITDA margin in 4Q23.
  • Net income of S/ 50.0 million, a 39.3% increase, mainly due to the impairment loss of our vertical kilns included in the 2023 results.
